title: monardella macrantha subsp. hallii
intro: Monardella macrantha subsp. hallii, also known as Hall's Mountain Monardella, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Native to the southwestern United States, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Monardella macrantha subsp. hallii include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
